description: STEMI presentation in a 65-year-old male diabetic with classic ACS red flags + concerning vitals.

patient.name: John M., 65M
patient.chief_complaint: Chest pain radiating to left arm with diaphoresis and nausea for 45 minutes. Pressure sensation. Worse with exertion.

rubric_notes: Classic ACS triad (chest pain + diaphoresis + radiation) + concerning vitals (SBP 88 borderline-critical, HR 112 concerning, SpO2 94 concerning) + high-risk demographics (65, diabetic, smoker). Per RUBRIC §3 ACS red flag mandates ESI 1-2. SBP 88 is at the boundary of concerning/critical — agent that calls this ESI 1 is defensible. Care area: resus minimum; trauma_bay acceptable.
